news.title: Bitcoin Dive Will Hike Strategy's Borrowing Rate To 11.75% — At Least
news.summary: Strategy's sale of $2.5 million bitcoin, which helped precipitate this week's dive for the cryptocurrency, appears certain to hike its annual financing costs by $26 million.  After sinking in heavy volume to start the month, Strategy's Stretch variable rate preferred stock with the STRC ticker has an impossibly steep mountain to climb to avoid a ratcheting up of its dividend rate by at least 25 basis points to 11.75% on all $10.5 billion of the outstanding securities.  Friday's even deeper selloff has put a potential 50-basis-point hike key Strategy's borrowing rate to 13% in play.

news.title: SpaceX IPO could turn Elon Musk into world's first trillionaire
news.summary: Tesla (TSLA) CEO Elon Musk is just one week away from his next big venture: the IPO of his space company SpaceX (SPAX.PVT). This event will be enough to push Musk's net worth over the edge and make him the world's first trillionaire. Yahoo Finance Senior Autos Reporter Pras Subramanian breaks down Musk's net worth and some of the risks that Wall Street is floating around the IPO. SpaceX is slated to begin trading publicly on the Nasdaq next Friday, June 12, aiming for an IPO price of $135 per share.

news.title: Tesla Stock Has One Less Sell Rating. J.P. Morgan Raises Its Price Target by 228%.
news.summary: Tesla  stock has one less Sell rating.  J.P. Morgan analyst Rajat Gupta on Friday upgraded Tesla to Hold from Sell.  Gupta recently took over coverage for Ryan Brinkman, who was a longtime Tesla bear.

news.title: Bitcoin Falls Below $60,000
news.summary: Bitcoin, the largest cryptocurrency, briefly dipped below $60,000 before staging a slight recovery. The largest cryptocurrency traded as low as $59,112 Friday, its lowest intraday level since October, 2024.

news.title: Nasdaq plunges 1,100 points over renewed rate hike concerns
news.summary: US stocks (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) sink in Friday trading, the Nasdaq Composite leading losses and closing over 1,100 points lower (4.18%). The market is reacting to May's overwhelmingly positive labor data — 172,000 jobs added the US economy vs. estimates of 88,000 — raising investor concerns that the Federal Reserve may be inclined to hike interest rates. Yahoo Finance Markets and Data Editor Jared Blikre breaks down the day's biggest market moves.
